meta.title: Traceronix — Network Diagnostics & IP Intelligence
meta.description: Port Checker, IP Lookup, DNS, WHOIS, SSL Inspection and Monitoring. Professional-grade network diagnostics in your browser.

nav.shortcut_footer: Tip: shortcuts only fire when no input is focused.

auth.signin_subtitle: Continue with your GitHub or Google account. We never request write access.

home.subtitle: Port checks, IP intelligence, DNS, WHOIS, SSL inspection and uptime monitoring — one fast platform, free to start.

features.title: Built for engineers, ready for everyone
features.subtitle: Fast, private, and accessible network tooling in your browser.
features.speed_body: Edge-routed checks deliver answers in milliseconds, not minutes.
features.privacy_body: No tracking pixels. Your queries are never sold or shared.
features.global_body: Multi-region probes give you a real picture of your network.
features.api_body: Every tool ships with a documented REST endpoint.

cta.title: Pin every tool you need on one dashboard.
cta.subtitle: Save favourites, get uptime alerts, and share results in seconds.

port-checker.title: Port Checker
port-checker.desc: Check TCP/UDP ports, scan ranges, detect services.
port-checker.meta_title: Port Checker — Scan TCP ports online
port-checker.meta_description: Check if a port is open on any server. Scan single ports, ranges, or common ports with latency and service detection.

explainer.purpose: The Port Checker tests whether a TCP port on a remote host is reachable from the public Internet. Use it to validate firewall rules, cloud security groups, local services behind NAT, and provider routing — no SSH or nmap on your machine required.
explainer.how_it_works:
The server opens a TCP connection to the target and reports open / closed / timeout.
Latency is measured in milliseconds so slow network hops are visible.
Common ports (21, 22, 80, 443, 3306, …) are annotated with the service hint.
explainer.when_to_use:
Verify firewall rules after provisioning a new server.
Reproduce a client's 'cannot connect' report without access to their network.
Check from the outside whether a service is publicly exposed (pre-pentest).
explainer.limits:
TCP only — UDP services like DNS, NTP, WireGuard cannot be probed.
No broad port-range scans (legally risky and likely blocked).

ip-lookup.title: IP Lookup
ip-lookup.meta_title: IP Lookup — Geolocation, ASN, VPN/Proxy detection
ip-lookup.meta_description: Look up any IP: country, city, ISP, ASN, timezone. Detect VPN, proxy, TOR, hosting.

explainer.purpose: IP Lookup aggregates geolocation, network and threat information for any public IPv4 or IPv6 address. It tells you the country, city, timezone, ISP, ASN, and whether the IP belongs to a VPN, proxy, TOR exit or hosting provider.
explainer.how_it_works:
Several geolocation APIs are queried in parallel; divergences are surfaced.
AS number and hosting detection come from BGP routing data.
TOR exits are matched against the official exit list; VPN/proxy is heuristic.
explainer.when_to_use:
Contextualise suspicious logins or sign-up attempts.
Roughly verify a customer's location for geo-pricing or compliance.
Trace abusive traffic back to proxy pools or hosting IPs.
explainer.limits:
City-level accuracy is imprecise — often just the ISP PoP, not the end user.
Mobile networks and CGNAT mask real locations.

dns-lookup.title: DNS Lookup
dns-lookup.desc: A, AAAA, MX, TXT, CNAME, NS records.
dns-lookup.meta_title: DNS Lookup — A, AAAA, MX, TXT, NS records
dns-lookup.meta_description: Fast DNS lookup for any domain. View A, AAAA, MX, TXT, CNAME, NS and CAA records.

explainer.purpose: DNS Lookup fetches every common resource record (A, AAAA, MX, TXT, CNAME, NS, SOA, CAA) for a domain in a single call. See what authoritative servers actually return without installing `dig` locally.
explainer.how_it_works:
Queries run over DNS-over-HTTPS (Cloudflare 1.1.1.1) — fast and tamper-resistant.
Multiple record types are loaded in parallel.
TTL and authority info is shown alongside.
explainer.when_to_use:
Verify a DNS change is live after you edited a zone.
Validate mail setup (MX, SPF in TXT, DKIM selectors).
Check CAA records before issuing certificates.
explainer.limits:
Returns data from one resolver — for global propagation use DNS Propagation.
Geo-DNS / anycast responses may vary by location.

dns-propagation.title: DNS Propagation
dns-propagation.meta_description: Check DNS propagation across 15+ public resolvers worldwide. See if your A, AAAA, MX, or TXT change is live everywhere.

explainer.purpose: DNS Propagation Check queries 15+ public DNS resolvers worldwide in parallel for the same record type and compares answers. Use it to see whether your DNS change has reached every corner of the Internet or only some regions.
explainer.how_it_works:
Parallel queries to Cloudflare, Google, Quad9, OpenDNS, Yandex, NextDNS and more.
Every answer is tabulated with its latency.
Divergent answers are color-highlighted.
explainer.when_to_use:
After a DNS change, verify it has propagated globally (respect TTL).
Validate geo-DNS / anycast configuration.
Spot resolver-level DNS hijacking (rare but possible).
explainer.limits:
Resolvers cache aggressively — high-TTL changes take a long time.
Public resolvers only — internal corporate DNS isn't accessible.

http-headers.title: HTTP Headers
http-headers.desc: Grade security headers A+ to F.
http-headers.meta_description: Grade your site's HTTP security headers A+ to F. Check HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options and more.

explainer.purpose: HTTP Headers Inspector fetches a URL and grades its security headers (HSTS, CSP, X-Frame-Options, X-Content-Type-Options, Referrer-Policy, Permissions-Policy) from A+ to F. See in seconds whether the site is hardened against XSS, clickjacking, and mixed-content attacks.
explainer.how_it_works:
Server sends a HEAD request and parses the response headers.
Each header is evaluated against current best practice (OWASP).
Non-security headers like Server and X-Powered-By are also shown.
explainer.when_to_use:
Validate header configuration before going live.
Security audit your own or a third-party web app.
Find missing HSTS or CSP gaps ahead of a pentest.
explainer.limits:
Grades standard headers only — custom security headers aren't credited.
CSP reports and nonce-based policies aren't actively tested.

tech-stack.title: Tech Stack
tech-stack.meta_title: Tech Stack Detector — What's this site built with?
tech-stack.meta_description: Detect frameworks, CMS, analytics, ecommerce, hosting and libraries from public response data.

explainer.purpose: Tech Stack Detector identifies frameworks, CMS, analytics, ecommerce, hosting and libraries from a site's public response data — HTML markup, HTTP headers, cookie names and JS globals. Popular for competitor research and lead qualification.
explainer.how_it_works:
HTML, headers and cookies are matched against 200+ known fingerprints.
Indicators are weighted with a confidence score.
Multiple hits in the same category (e.g. 2 analytics) are shown.
explainer.when_to_use:
Analyze a competitor's tech stack before pitching them.
B2B lead qualification (who uses Shopify? who doesn't yet?).
Deprecation audit: is this site running an ancient jQuery?
explainer.limits:
Bot-protected sites (Cloudflare challenge) return no usable HTML — empty results are expected.
Client-only SPAs reveal their stack only after JS execution.

redirects.title: Redirect Tracer
redirects.desc: Follow every hop, spot loops and downgrades.
redirects.meta_description: Trace every hop of a URL's redirect chain. Spot loops, HTTPS downgrades, and too-many-redirects that hurt SEO.

explainer.purpose: Redirect Tracer follows a URL through every HTTP redirect hop, logging status code, target URL and latency. Spot loops, HTTPS downgrades, and too-many-redirects that hurt SEO and performance.
explainer.how_it_works:
The server manually follows every 3xx until a final 2xx response.
A hard cap of 20 hops prevents infinite loops.
Protocol and host changes are visually highlighted.
explainer.when_to_use:
Site migration: verify old URLs redirect correctly to the new structure.
SEO audit: Google penalizes chains with 4+ hops.
Security audit: detect HTTP→HTTPS downgrades as an XSS vector.
explainer.limits:
Follows server 3xx only — JS-based (window.location) or meta-refresh aren't detected.
Cookie-gated redirects (login walls) hit a wall.

subdomains.title: Subdomain Finder
subdomains.meta_description: Enumerate subdomains from public Certificate Transparency logs. Fast, passive, no scanning.

explainer.purpose: Subdomain Finder enumerates a domain's subdomains from public Certificate Transparency (CT) logs. Because every SSL certificate ever issued is logged in CT, those logs reveal almost every subdomain that ever existed — entirely passively, no scanning.
explainer.how_it_works:
crt.sh is queried as the primary CT-log source.
If crt.sh is down, CertSpotter (SSLMate) serves as a reliable backup.
Results are deduplicated and grouped by depth below the apex.
explainer.when_to_use:
Asset discovery before a pentest or bug bounty.
Identify forgotten staging/dev subdomains before an attacker does.
M&A due diligence: what subdomains does the target run?
explainer.limits:
Only finds subdomains that ever had a public SSL certificate.
Pure wildcard certificates (*.example.com) don't reveal concrete hosts.

cdn-detector.title: CDN Detector
cdn-detector.desc: Identify Cloudflare, Fastly, Akamai and more.
cdn-detector.meta_title: CDN Detector — What CDN is this site using?
cdn-detector.meta_description: Detect Cloudflare, Fastly, Akamai, CloudFront, Vercel, Netlify and 12+ other CDNs.

explainer.purpose: CDN Detector identifies which content-delivery network a domain uses — Cloudflare, Fastly, Akamai, CloudFront, Vercel, Netlify and more. Useful for performance analysis, pentest reconnaissance, and competitor research.
explainer.how_it_works:
Multiple signals are combined: HTTP headers (Server, X-Cache, Via), CNAME target, IP WHOIS.
Each CDN has unique fingerprints our database recognizes.
Multi-layer setups (e.g. Cloudflare in front of Fastly) are detected.
explainer.when_to_use:
Performance analysis: which CDN serves the site?
Before a pentest: identify the WAF (Cloudflare, Imperva).
Analyze a competitor's tech stack.
explainer.limits:
Some CDNs obscure their identity (custom CNAMEs, generic headers).
Only the outermost layer is visible — origin server stays hidden.

ssl-check.title: SSL Certificate
ssl-check.meta_description: Inspect SSL/TLS certificates: issuer, chain, expiry, cipher suite and TLS version.

explainer.purpose: SSL Certificate Check fetches the certificate from a TLS host and shows the issuer, chain, validity, SAN list, cipher suite and negotiated TLS version. Use it to spot misconfigurations, expired certificates and missing intermediates.
explainer.how_it_works:
A TLS handshake on port 443 (or custom) returns the server certificate.
The full chain up to the root CA is walked and validated.
Fingerprints (SHA-1/SHA-256) and public-key type are reported.
explainer.when_to_use:
Verify wildcard or SAN coverage before a deploy.
Proactively warn before expiry (automate with monitoring).
Reproduce browser warnings (hostname mismatch, self-signed).
explainer.limits:
Only works against TCP hosts on standard TLS ports.
Client authentication (mTLS) is not performed.

whois.title: WHOIS / RDAP
whois.meta_description: Modern RDAP-based domain registration lookup: registrar, status, nameservers, dates.

explainer.purpose: WHOIS / RDAP reveals a domain's registration data: registrar, status, nameservers, creation and expiry dates. The modern RDAP API returns structured JSON instead of the legacy free-text WHOIS format.
explainer.how_it_works:
The query is dispatched to rdap.org or the TLD-specific RDAP server.
Fields like status, events, entities are normalized into a readable form.
Falls back to classic WHOIS when RDAP is unavailable for a TLD.
explainer.when_to_use:
Identify a domain owner for outreach or acquisition.
Get ahead of an expiring domain you depend on.
Report an abusive domain by finding the abuse contact.
explainer.limits:
Privacy services obscure ownership — you'll see only the proxy.
WHOIS data is not guaranteed up-to-date or accurate (GDPR).

reachability.title: Reachability
reachability.desc: HTTP, TCP, ping and global status.
reachability.meta_description: Test HTTP, TCP and ping reachability to any server, with response time.

explainer.purpose: Reachability Test probes three layers in parallel: HTTP response, TCP connect, and ICMP ping. You can tell whether a server is fully offline, only the web service is down, or the firewall is filtering ICMP.
explainer.how_it_works:
Three parallel probes from the server: ping, TCP connect, HTTP GET.
Latency is measured per layer.
Short status codes (DNS error, timeout, refused) are translated to English.
explainer.when_to_use:
Quick availability diagnosis without SSH into the data center.
Tell apart 'internet routing is broken' from 'only the app is down'.
Detect ICMP filtering — many cloud providers block pings.
explainer.limits:
Tested from one server location — for a global view you need multiple probes.
No continuous observation; only a snapshot.

email-verify.title: Email Verifier
email-verify.meta_title: Email Verifier — MX, disposable, SMTP probe
email-verify.meta_description: Verify any email address: syntax, MX records, disposable provider check, optional SMTP handshake.

explainer.purpose: Email Verifier tests whether an email address is valid and deliverable — syntax, MX records, disposable provider, and optional SMTP handshake. Prevents invalid sign-ups and newsletter bounces.
explainer.how_it_works:
Syntax is checked against RFC 5322.
The domain's MX records are resolved — missing means undeliverable.
Disposable providers are matched against a curated list; SMTP probe confirms deliverability.
explainer.when_to_use:
Validate newsletter sign-ups inline at the form.
Clean old mailing lists before a campaign.
Block throwaway addresses on signup.
explainer.limits:
SMTP probes are increasingly blocked by mail servers.
Catch-all domains accept any address — verdict is inconclusive there.

email-auth.title: SPF / DMARC / DKIM
email-auth.meta_description: Check email authentication records for any domain. See SPF policy, DMARC enforcement and DKIM setup with warnings.

explainer.purpose: SPF / DKIM / DMARC Analyzer audits a domain's email authentication — three standards that together prevent attackers from sending mail in your name (spoofing, phishing). Outputs a grade with concrete improvement suggestions.
explainer.how_it_works:
Reads TXT records: v=spf1, _dmarc.‹domain›, default._domainkey.‹domain›.
SPF is checked against the 10-lookup limit; DMARC for p= and rua=.
DKIM selectors are probed with common names (default, google, mail).
explainer.when_to_use:
Audit your own mail authentication before a critical newsletter campaign.
Verify DMARC enforcement (p=quarantine/reject) is in effect after setup.
Diagnose spoofing problems on customer domains.
explainer.limits:
DKIM-selector detection is heuristic; custom selectors may be missed.
Does not test actual send paths — only configuration is graded.

blacklist.title: IP Blacklist
blacklist.desc: Check an IP on 20+ spam DNSBLs.
blacklist.meta_description: Check if an IP is on 20+ major spam blacklists (Spamhaus, Barracuda, SORBS). Instant, parallel DNSBL queries.

explainer.purpose: IP Blacklist Check tests an IP against 20+ well-known spam DNSBLs (Spamhaus, Barracuda, SORBS, SpamCop, …) in parallel. Hits include the listing reason. Important for mail-server operators and IT forensics.
explainer.how_it_works:
One DNS lookup per list using reversed-IP under the DNSBL domain.
All lists are queried in parallel with a 3 s timeout each.
Resolved answers often carry a code revealing the listing reason.
explainer.when_to_use:
When your mail is being rejected as spam — check whether your mail IP is listed.
Re-check after an IP change at your ISP / cloud provider.
Check suspicious IPs from logs against Spamhaus.
explainer.limits:
A 'clean' IP isn't a guarantee — some lists are private.
Listings may be cached briefly (DNSBL TTL).

jwt.title: JWT Decoder
jwt.meta_title: JWT Decoder — Inspect JSON Web Tokens
jwt.meta_description: Paste a JWT to decode header and payload, inspect claims, and verify expiry — entirely in your browser, nothing sent to servers.

explainer.purpose: JWT Decoder decodes JSON Web Tokens (header + payload) and shows claims (sub, exp, iat, iss, aud …) in human-readable form. Runs entirely in the browser — your token never leaves the device and is not sent to any server.
explainer.how_it_works:
The token is split on the two dots into three base64url segments.
Header and payload are decoded client-side.
Expiry (exp) is compared against current time and visualized.
explainer.when_to_use:
Inspect claims while debugging auth flows in your own apps.
Analyze a customer's token without privacy worries (nothing leaves the page).
Quick check whether a refresh token has expired.
explainer.limits:
Does NOT verify the signature — you need the issuer's public key for that.
Only JWS (signed JWTs); JWE (encrypted) is not decrypted.

password-leak.title: Password Leak
password-leak.desc: HIBP check with k-anonymity — never leaves browser.
password-leak.meta_description: Check if your password appears in known data breaches. Uses Have I Been Pwned's k-anonymity API — only the first 5 SHA-1 characters are ever sent.

explainer.purpose: Password Leak Check tells you whether your password appears in known breaches (3B+ entries). Uses Have I Been Pwned's k-anonymity API — only the first 5 characters of the SHA-1 hash are sent, your password never leaves the browser.
explainer.how_it_works:
SHA-1 of the password is computed in the browser.
Only the first 5 hash characters are sent to api.pwnedpasswords.com.
Server returns ALL hashes with that prefix; comparison happens client-side.
explainer.when_to_use:
Check a new password before adopting it.
Audit old passwords for compromise.
Security-awareness training (live demo of breach count).
explainer.limits:
Hash match only — variants (Pa$$word vs password) hash separately.
No zero-day coverage; reported breaches can be months old.

dnssec.title: DNSSEC Validator
dnssec.meta_description: Check DNSSEC deployment for any domain. See DS, DNSKEY and RRSIG records with warnings.

explainer.purpose: DNSSEC Validator checks whether a domain has DNSSEC deployed correctly — DS records at the parent zone, DNSKEY records, RRSIG signatures and the chain of trust to the root KSK. Protects against DNS spoofing and cache poisoning.
explainer.how_it_works:
Full DNSSEC resolution with AD-bit verification.
DS, DNSKEY and RRSIG are fetched individually and checked against an algorithm whitelist.
Missing or weak algorithms (RSA-MD5, SHA-1) trigger warnings.
explainer.when_to_use:
Verify signatures are valid before / after enabling DNSSEC.
Monitor key rollovers — old and new DNSKEYs must overlap.
Diagnose registrar-side DS submission problems.
explainer.limits:
Only reports validation status; repairs are made at the registrar.
Does NOT validate subdomains self-signed without a parent DS.

cookies.title: Cookie / GDPR
cookies.meta_description: Inspect Set-Cookie flags and third-party trackers loaded on any page. GDPR risk score.

explainer.purpose: Cookie & GDPR Analyzer loads a URL and lists every cookie set, including security flags (Secure, HttpOnly, SameSite, Max-Age) plus third-party trackers detected. Helps with GDPR compliance and privacy audits.
explainer.how_it_works:
Browser-like fetch collects every Set-Cookie header on the response.
Third-party domains are mapped against a curated tracker / ads / analytics list.
Missing security flags are reported with the reason they matter.
explainer.when_to_use:
GDPR audit your own site before a legal review.
Spot suspicious trackers on competitor sites.
Find misconfiguration (missing HttpOnly, SameSite=None without Secure).
explainer.limits:
Cookies set only after user interaction (consent banner) are missed.
Not a full browser — complex JS-driven trackers may slip past.

opengraph.title: OG Preview
opengraph.desc: Social share preview for any URL.
opengraph.meta_description: Preview how your URL looks when shared on Twitter, Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack.

explainer.purpose: OpenGraph & Meta Preview shows how your URL will look when shared on Twitter/X, Facebook, LinkedIn, Slack and Discord. Handy for debugging OG tags before you post.
explainer.how_it_works:
Server fetches the HTML and parses every og: and twitter: meta tag.
Missing essential tags (og:title, og:image, og:description) are reported.
Preview cards for multiple platforms are simulated.
explainer.when_to_use:
Test the preview before publishing an important post.
Debug why Twitter / Facebook doesn't show an image card.
Audit a whole site for consistent OG tags.
explainer.limits:
Server-rendered tags only — JS apps setting OG client-side aren't evaluated.
Real platform crawlers cache; changes may need a re-scrape.

robots.title: Robots & Sitemap
robots.meta_description: Parse and validate robots.txt rules and sitemap XML for any host.

explainer.purpose: Robots.txt & Sitemap Validator parses a domain's robots.txt, groups all Allow/Disallow rules by user agent, and validates linked sitemap XMLs against the schema. Important for crawl-budget management and SEO.
explainer.how_it_works:
robots.txt is parsed per RFC 9309; every line is syntax-checked.
Sitemap URLs are fetched and validated as well-formed XML.
Contradictions (sitemap blocked by robots.txt!) are flagged.
explainer.when_to_use:
Before launch, verify search engines index the right things.
Analyze a competitor's robots.txt (what are they blocking?).
Debug why Google Search Console reports pages as 'blocked'.
explainer.limits:
Syntax validation only — we don't test whether crawlers actually respect the rules.
No deep sitemap crawl; only the top-level XML is validated.

ipv6.title: IPv6 Readiness
ipv6.meta_description: Grade your domain's IPv6 deployment across apex, www, nameservers, and mail.

explainer.purpose: IPv6 Readiness Score grades how well a domain is prepared for IPv6 — apex (example.com), www, name servers and MX servers are each checked for AAAA records. Important for mobile networks and IPv6 compliance with governments / telcos.
explainer.how_it_works:
Four layers are checked: apex AAAA, www AAAA, NS AAAA, MX AAAA.
Each present layer scores 25 points; total ranges 0–100.
Missing layers are annotated with a fix hint.
explainer.when_to_use:
Document the status quo before an IPv6 migration.
Government / compliance audits (BSI baseline, US Federal IPv6).
Prioritise which layer to make IPv6-ready first.
explainer.limits:
Only checks DNS existence, not actual IPv6 reachability.
Wildcard AAAAs are found but not credited as 'real' readiness.

bgp.title: BGP / ASN
bgp.meta_description: Look up the BGP prefix, announcing ASNs and route of any IP. Or inspect an ASN's announced prefixes.

explainer.purpose: BGP Lookup queries the global routing tables to show which AS announces a given IP, which prefixes an AS holds, and which neighbour ASes it peers with. Data comes live from RIPEstat — the same source network engineers use for routing diagnostics.
explainer.how_it_works:
Queries hit stat.ripe.net (RIPE NCC), which has a full routing view.
For an IP, the most-specific announced prefix and the origin AS are returned.
For an AS, you get every announced prefix and its peering list.
explainer.when_to_use:
Distinguish cloud providers from residential ISPs (hosting detection).
Diagnose route hijacks or unintended re-announcements.
Check global visibility of your own prefix.
explainer.limits:
Routing data is snapshotted; very recent changes take minutes to surface.
No full AS-path visualisation (only direct neighbours).

mixed-content.title: Mixed Content
mixed-content.desc: Find http:// resources on HTTPS pages.
mixed-content.meta_description: Find insecure http:// resources on your HTTPS pages that browsers block or warn about.

explainer.purpose: Mixed Content Scan finds http:// resources embedded on an HTTPS page (images, scripts, iframes, fonts) — which browsers either block outright or warn about. These cause broken layouts, missing images, and security warnings.
explainer.how_it_works:
The server loads the HTML and parses every src / href attribute.
Each http:// URL is classified: blocking (scripts, iframes) or warning (images, fonts).
CSS imports and @font-face declarations are also inspected.
explainer.when_to_use:
Before migrating a legacy backend to HTTPS, find which resources need updating.
After installing a third-party plugin, check whether it injects http calls.
Reproduce Google Search Console warnings.
explainer.limits:
Static HTML only — JS-injected http resources aren't found.
First level only; iframes are not followed.

dashboard.title: My IP
dashboard.desc: Your IP, location, ISP, browser, OS.
dashboard.meta_description: Your IP, location, ISP, browser, OS, screen, timezone — all in one view.

guard.blocked_localhost: Localhost and loopback addresses are not allowed. Try a public hostname or IP.
guard.blocked_private: Private network addresses (RFC 1918) cannot be diagnosed from a public tool.
guard.blocked_link_local: Link-local addresses are not reachable from a public diagnostic tool.
guard.blocked_metadata: Cloud-provider metadata endpoints are blocked for safety.
guard.blocked_reserved_tld: This top-level domain is reserved (e.g. .local, .test) and never resolves publicly.
guard.invalid_target: That doesn't look like a valid hostname or IP.

common.error: Something went wrong. Please try again.
common.input_required: Please fill in this field before submitting.
common.invalid_host_shape: doesn't look like a hostname or IP

not_found.title: Page not found
not_found.desc: The page you're looking for doesn't exist or has been moved.
not_found.hint: Double-check the URL or head back to the dashboard.
not_found.search_hint: Looking for a tool? Try the search above.

cdn.title: CDN Detector
cdn.subtitle: Identify the CDN or WAF in front of any host.

dashboard.title: My IP Dashboard
dashboard.subtitle: Everything we can detect about your connection.

dns.subtitle: Query DNS records for any domain.

email_auth.title: SPF / DKIM / DMARC
email_auth.subtitle: Parse and grade email authentication for any domain.

email_verify.title: Email Verifier

email_verify.smtp_label: Perform SMTP RCPT probe (slower, may trigger spam filters on target server)
email_verify.no_mx: No MX records — domain cannot receive mail.

headers.title: HTTP Security Headers
headers.subtitle: Analyze response headers and grade security posture from A+ to F.

ip.title: IP Lookup
ip.subtitle: Geolocation, ASN, ISP and threat intelligence.
ip.multi_subtitle: Same IP queried in parallel against multiple geolocation APIs — divergences reveal data-source differences.

ipv6.title: IPv6 Readiness
ipv6.subtitle: AAAA coverage across apex, www, NS and MX.
ipv6.nxdomain_message: We couldn't resolve any DNS records for {domain}. The domain likely doesn't exist.
ipv6.nxdomain_hint_typo: Double-check the spelling of the domain name.
ipv6.nxdomain_hint_scheme: Remove any http:// or https:// prefix and enter only the domain.
ipv6.nxdomain_hint_resolver: Try again — public DNS resolvers can occasionally return inconsistent answers.

jwt.title: JWT Decoder
jwt.subtitle: Paste any JWT — decoded entirely in your browser. Nothing leaves your device.

mixed.subtitle: Find http:// resources on HTTPS pages.

opengraph.title: OG Preview
opengraph.subtitle: Social share preview for any URL.

password.title: Password Leak Check
password.subtitle: HIBP k-anonymity check — your password never leaves the browser.
password.safe: Not found in any known breach
password.safe_note: This password hash isn't on HIBP's list — but that doesn't guarantee it's strong.
password.unsafe_note: Change this password everywhere you use it. Attackers have seen this exact password.
password.privacy_note: SHA-1 happens in your browser. Only the first 5 hash characters are sent to HIBP.

ports.title: Port Checker
ports.subtitle: Check TCP port reachability, scan common ports, or sweep a range.
ports.invalid_range: Range start must be ≤ end, both within 1-65535.
ports.range_too_wide: Range too wide — please scan at most 1024 ports at a time.

reachability.title: Reachability
reachability.subtitle: HTTP, TCP, ping and global status check.

redirects.title: Redirect Tracer
redirects.subtitle: Follow every redirect hop, detect loops and HTTPS downgrades.

robots.title: Robots & Sitemap
robots.subtitle: Parse robots.txt and validate referenced sitemaps.

ssl.title: SSL Certificate Inspector
ssl.subtitle: TLS chain, expiry, cipher suite and grade.

subdomains.title: Subdomain Finder
subdomains.subtitle: Passive enumeration via CT logs.
subdomains.input_hint: Accepts full URLs (https://example.com/foo) — scheme, path, port and "www." are stripped automatically.
subdomains.degraded_message: crt.sh is currently unreachable or rate-limiting us. Try again in a minute — this does NOT mean the domain has no subdomains.
subdomains.truncated_notice: Result capped — there are more subdomains we didn't return

tech.title: Tech Stack Detector
tech.subtitle: Detect frameworks, CMS, analytics and hosting provider.
tech.empty_reasons: The site likely has bot protection (Cloudflare challenge, captcha) or returned a minimal HTML page (login wall, SPA shell). This does NOT mean the site uses no technologies — try a public marketing page instead of an app/login URL.
